title: Make candidate source commit lifecycle explicit
outOfScope: Changing release tag creation, provider asset generation, or publication ordering
Publishing a release or moving an existing tag

sources.reason: Defines the Candidate Source Commit lifecycle problem and acceptance.
Current workflow requires a manually supplied source_commit and compares it with the default branch tip.
Existing workflow contract tests define exact-SHA release behavior.
Canonical candidate metadata contains the preparation source snapshot.
Current English release identity and candidate documentation.
Current Japanese release identity and candidate documentation.

riskAssessment.reason: The workflow must never release a stale or caller-selected commit; the change is limited to source selection and its evidence.

executionDecision.reason: The requested lifecycle model is deterministic: compute the default branch commit at workflow start and treat an optional input only as an assertion.

preReviewWarnings: Review that the optional source_commit input cannot override the freshly resolved default branch commit.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record pre-edit and pre-finish evidence for source identity behavior.

acceptance: Release workflow computes sourceCommit from the freshly resolved default branch when dispatch input is omitted.
A supplied source_commit is accepted only when it exactly equals the freshly resolved default branch commit; stale or mismatched values fail before checkout or publication.
The workflow writes the resolved commit, remote, default branch, and run identity to release-source.json, and tests assert those fields use the freshly resolved default branch commit.
English and Japanese distribution documentation describe the computed source identity, exact-SHA checks, and fail-closed behavior.
guidelines: New public APIs must be documented.
Do not import internal modules from other features.

intent.problem: A candidate sourceCommit recorded before its PR merges becomes stale as soon as the candidate PR itself changes main, creating a self-invalidating release candidate and ambiguous release identity.
constraints: Preserve exact-SHA checkout, smoke, compatibility, evidence, tag, and release ordering.
Do not publish, move, or delete tags.
Do not allow a workflow caller to select a commit other than the current default branch tip.
intent.rationale: Resolve the source at the start of the release workflow and retain the explicit input only as a fail-closed assertion, so the candidate cannot authorize its own stale commit.

rawUserRequest: 执行评审整改计划工单 2：解决 Candidate Source Commit 生命周期问题，继续循环执行直到全部工单完成。

declaredIntent.summary: Resolve the release source commit from the current default branch at workflow start and reject stale explicit assertions.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: stale or mismatched explicit source input

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized changes to workflow, tests, governance checks, and documentation in the execution plan.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item commit and restore the required source_commit input behavior.
